prospect.Summary: After three seasons at Division III Southwestern (TX), Smith transferred to the University of Arizona and enjoyed a velocity spike throughout his only season there, with some 88 mph fastballs at the start of the year and some 98 mph heaters at the end. In 2022, he continued to sit 94-97 and touch at least 98 while closing at Low-A Rome. He missed 2023 due to a UCL tear and walked a batter per inning in 2024, but has recaptured viable feel for location in 2025. At the time he was traded to Colorado Smith had a 1.24 WHIP, 26% K% and 11% BB% at Double-A Mississippi. Smith's huge, slightly open stride down the mound generates just over seven feet of extension, and his fastball sits 95-plus. That pitch is playing like a premium offering this year, Smith is throwing harder than ever. His slider is more average, and fairly well located given how much effort Smith pitches with. He looks like a soon-to-be middle reliever.
